query: CRT television static screen flicker old TV monitor
message: anti-aesthetic / clarity_and_focus / analog_degradation: CRT television static and old TV monitors

response.objective_caption: A close-up of a human eye rendered through vertical RGB pixel stripes, where individual red, green, and blue subpixels create a screen-door effect across the entire image. The eye appears in partial profile view with visible iris, sclera, and a bright reflection or catchlight, while fine skin texture and eyebrow hairs are decomposed into discrete colored lines. The composition fills the frame with the eye centered amid the repeating vertical phosphor elements.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A close-up of a human eye heavily distorted by vertical RGB pixel scanlines that decompose the image into discrete red, green, and blue phosphor stripes, evoking aged CRT monitor or VHS display decay. The analog degradation renders fine details as smeared vertical bands, with extreme chromatic aberration separating colors along high-contrast edges and creating fringing around the iris and skin tones. The naturalistic eye becomes partially abstract through the screen-door interference pattern, producing a surreal, dreamlike effect where the organic subject dissolves into mechanical display texture, collapsing representational clarity into pure color-channel verticality and retro monitor surface noise.
